message.content:
Adversarial verifier, HC-GRC agent/pipeline deep audit. Repo /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc. REFUTE this candidate by reading the real code AND the cited spec. Remember: an intentional Phase-0/1 stub (NotImplementedError + IMPLEMENTED=False) is NOT a defect — set is_stub_by_design=true and confirmed=false for those. Default confirmed=false unless it is a genuine defect in implemented logic, a real research-integrity hole, or a real code-vs-spec contradiction.

CANDIDATE (unit embedding-agent (src/agents/embedding_agent/agent.py)):
title: Docstring describes incorrect Phase 1 evaluation criterion
severity: medium
location: src/agents/embedding_agent/agent.py:110-111
problem: The docstring states 'Phase 1 runs all 5 candidates (3 general + 2 diversity anchors) and records F1 scores to inform Gate 2 model selection.' This misrepresents the Phase 1 evaluation process. According to SAP §10 (Phase 1 — Benchmark evaluation), Phase 1 candidates are evaluated on: (a) STS-B and SICK-R benchmarks, (b) regulatory domain benchmarks, and (c) SCF structural metadata anchor pairs (STRM '= Equal To' and '∅ No Relation' pairs). The final F1 evaluation on STRM classification is performed by the P1 Agent after EDA (Phase 2), not Phase 1. Phase 2 uses the validation split to compute Silhouette score, Anchor consistency score, and Cross-model agreement, with the primary model designated by highest composite score at Gate 2.
spec_or_invariant: docs/protocol/03_statistical_analysis_plan.md §10 (Model Selection Criteria) — specifies Phase 1 benchmark evaluation vs. Phase 2 calibration sample evaluation. The agent docstring should align with this two-phase protocol.
proposed_fix: Revise line 110-111 to accurately describe Phase 1: 'Phase 1 evaluates all 5 candidates (3 general + 2 diversity anchors) on benchmarks and SCF structural metadata anchor pairs (per SAP §10 Phase 1) to produce a ranked shortlist for Phase 2 calibration.' Then clarify the post-Phase-2 F1 evaluation in line 121-122 as part of the Gate 2 decision process.

Verify against actual code + spec (read-only). Cite file:line and the exact spec clause. Be honest about severity.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll verify this candidate by examining the actual code and the statistical analysis plan specification.

content.content:
1	"""
2	EmbeddingAgent — Dense vector infrastructure for SCF controls and STRM mappings.
3	
4	Generates embeddings from the curated corpus, stores them in Qdrant (semantic
5	search, all agents), and builds the FAISS flat index (P1 all-pairs computation).
6	These are complementary — do not collapse into a single index.
7	
8	Model selection is governed by LEDGER-0002 (model candidates pre-registered) and
9	configs/platform.yaml (model_candidates section). Primary model designation is a
10	Gate 2 event. All candidates in the LEDGER-0002 race must be evaluated in Phase 1
11	before designation.
12	
13	If selected model F1 < 0.70 on STRM classification: triggers domain model
14	fine-tuning via Team 06 (human approval required).
15	
16	Phase 1 stub: run() raises NotImplementedError.
17	
18	Required tools: mcp-qdrant, mcp-dvc, mcp-lab-notebook
19	Required skills: sentence-transformers, qdrant
20	See: agents/02-data/embedding-agent/AGENT.md
21	"""
22	
23	from __future__ import annotations
24	
25	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
26	from typing import Any
27	
28	from ...state import HCGRCState
29	from ..base import BasePipelineAgent
30	
31	# ── Result types ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
32	
33	
34	@dataclass
35	class EmbeddingManifest:
36	    """
37	    Provenance record for an embedding run. Written to state and to disk.
38	    Required at Gate 2 to confirm which model produced the embeddings.
39	    """
40	
41	    model_name: str = ""           # HuggingFace model ID, e.g. "BAAI/bge-base-en-v1.5"
42	    model_hash: str = ""           # SHA-256 of model weights file
43	    corpus_dvc_hash: str = ""      # DVC hash of curated corpus used for embedding
44	    timestamp_utc: str = ""
45	    control_embedding_count: int = 0
46	    mapping_embedding_count: int = 0
47	    qdrant_collections: list[str] = field(
48	        default_factory=lambda: ["hcgrc_controls", "hcgrc_mappings"]
49	    )
50	    faiss_index_path: str = ""     # data/03-processed/faiss/ (DVC tracked)
51	    faiss_index_dvc_hash: str = ""
52	    strm_f1_score: float | None = None  # populated by P1 after evaluation; None pre-evaluation
53	
54	
55	@dataclass
56	class EmbeddingQualityReport:
57	    """Spot-check results for nearest-neighbor coherence. Surfaced in lab notebook."""
58	
59	    sample_size: int = 50          # 50 samples per AGENT.md evaluation criteria
60	    coherent_count: int = 0
61	    incoherent_examples: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
62	    mean_intracluster_similarity: float = 0.0
63	    oov_rate: float = 0.0          # out-of-vocabulary token rate
64	    p95_query_latency_ms: float = 0.0  # Qdrant semantic search
65	
66	
67	# F1 threshold below which domain fine-tuning is triggered (AGENT.md)
68	_STRM_F1_THRESHOLD = 0.70
69	
70	
71	# ── Agent ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
72	
73	
74	class EmbeddingAgent(BasePipelineAgent):
75	    """
76	    Generates and manages the vector infrastructure for all HC-GRC semantic analysis.
77	
78	    FAISS index: for P1's exhaustive all-pairs computation (~280K × 280K pairwise
79	    comparisons). Read-only after P1 analysis begins.
80	
81	    Qdrant collections: for semantic search by P2, P3, P4, P5, and Literature Agent.
82	    Supports hybrid search (dense + sparse), metadata filtering by SCF domain/
83	    framework/maturity level, and scroll-based bulk retrieval.
84	
85	    Behavioral constraints (per AGENT.md):
86	      - Never mix embeddings from different model versions in the same index
87	      - Embedding manifest updated before any analysis agent queries the index
88	      - FAISS index is read-only after P1 analysis begins
89	      - All stochastic components use seed from configs/seeds.yaml
90	      - Embedding drift: if mean cosine shift > 0.05 on new SCF version, flag
91	        for human review — never auto-re-embed
92	      - Never export Qdrant collections or FAISS indices off-machine (CC BY-ND 4.0)
93	    """
94	
95	    AGENT_ID = "embedding-agent"
96	    PROTECTED = False
97	
98	    # Drift threshold: if mean cosine similarity shift exceeds this after SCF update,
99	    # flag for human review rather than auto-re-embedding
100	    DRIFT_THRESHOLD = 0.05
101	
102	    def run(self, state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
103	        """
104	        Generate embeddings, build Qdrant collections and FAISS index.
105	
106	        Phase 1 implementation will:
107	          1. Load primary model candidate from configs/platform.yaml
108	             (model_candidates section — LEDGER-0002 candidate set)
109	             NOTE: primary model is NULL until Gate 2 designation. Phase 1 runs
110	             all 5 candidates (3 general + 2 diversity anchors) and records F1
111	             scores to inform Gate 2 model selection.
112	          2. Batch-encode curated control text (GPU if available, CPU fallback)
113	          3. Build Qdrant collection: hcgrc_controls (1,400+ controls)
114	          4. Build Qdrant collection: hcgrc_mappings (280K+ STRM pair embeddings)
115	          5. Build FAISS flat L2 index for P1 all-pairs (~280K × embedding_dim)
116	          6. Run quality spot-check: 50 nearest-neighbor samples, measure coherence
117	          7. Record EmbeddingManifest to state (embedding_manifest field)
118	          8. DVC push FAISS index to local remote
119	          9. Append embedding quality report to lab notebook
120	
121	        If any model achieves F1 < 0.70 on STRM classification (evaluated by
122	        P1 agent after EDA), triggers domain fine-tuning via Team 06 + human
123	        approval before Gate 2 model designation.
124	
125	        Returns partial HCGRCState update:
126	          embedding_manifest: EmbeddingManifest as dict
127	          phase_1_ready: True — data pipeline complete, P1-P5 may start
128	          prov_activities: embedding provenance record
129	        """
130	        raise NotImplementedError(
131	            "EmbeddingAgent.run() — Phase 1 implementation pending. "
132	            "Requires: curated corpus from DataCurationAgent, Qdrant Docker running, "
133	            "sentence-transformers + FAISS installed, configs/platform.yaml with "
134	            "LEDGER-0002 model candidates. See agents/02-data/embedding-agent/AGENT.md."
135	        )
136	
137	    def check_f1_threshold(self, manifest: EmbeddingManifest) -> bool:
138	        """
139	        Returns True if strm_f1_score meets the minimum threshold.
140	
141	        If False: triggers domain model fine-tuning request via Team 06.
142	        Called by P1 agent after EDA evaluation; result recorded to manifest.
143	        """
144	        if manifest.strm_f1_score is None:
145	            return True  # Not yet evaluated — allow to proceed
146	        return manifest.strm_f1_score >= _STRM_F1_THRESHOLD
147	
148	    def check_embedding_drift(
149	        self, mean_cosine_shift: float
150	    ) -> bool:
151	        """
152	        Returns True if cosine similarity distribution shift is within threshold.
153	
154	        If False: flag for human review. Never auto-re-embed on drift — requires
155	        protocol review to determine if drift affects pre-registered analysis.
156	        """
157	        return mean_cosine_shift <= self.DRIFT_THRESHOLD
158	
159	    def _build_prov_record(
160	        self, manifest: EmbeddingManifest, run_id: str
161	    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
162	        """Build provenance record for the embedding run."""
163	        return {
164	            "activity": "embedding_generation",
165	            "agent_id": self.AGENT_ID,
166	            "run_id": run_id,
167	            "model_name": manifest.model_name,
168	            "model_hash": manifest.model_hash,
169	            "corpus_dvc_hash": manifest.corpus_dvc_hash,
170	            "timestamp_utc": manifest.timestamp_utc,
171	            "control_embedding_count": manifest.control_embedding_count,
172	            "mapping_embedding_count": manifest.mapping_embedding_count,
173	            "faiss_index_dvc_hash": manifest.faiss_index_dvc_hash,
174	        }
175

1	# Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP)
2	
3	> **DEFERRAL NOTICE — 2026-06-10**
4	> This document is a template. The SAP is finalized at Gate 2, after exploratory characterization of the SCF corpus. Fields marked `[To be populated]` are structurally deferred — populating them before exploratory analysis runs would constitute pre-specification of effect sizes without empirical foundation.
5	>
6	> **Locked now (framework pre-registration):** Analytical workflow, hypothesis format, correction methods, split strategy (GroupKFold on SCF control IDs per issue #60), unit of analysis (SCF control, ~1,400 effective observations per issue #67), and model selection criteria (Phase 1/2 protocol per ADR-0013).
7	>
8	> **Deferred to Gate 2:** Specific hypotheses, effect size thresholds, primary outcome measures, multiple comparison families.
9	
10	**Version**: [version]  
11	**Date locked**: [date — this document is immutable after this date]  
12	**Pre-registration record**: `protocol/registration/`  
13	
14	This document specifies every analysis this project will conduct. It is written and committed before any confirmatory analysis runs. Analyses not specified here that are conducted after data examination are exploratory and must be labeled as such in all reporting.
15	
16	The SAP is a single document. It is not a collection of hypothesis files. Every analysis — primary, secondary, sensitivity, and exploratory — is listed here in one place so the full scope of the analytical program is visible and auditable.
17	
18	---
19	
20	## 1. Primary Analysis
21	
22	The primary analysis is the one the project is fundamentally designed to answer. There is only one. If a project has multiple primary analyses, it has multiple projects.
23	
24	**Primary question**: [To be populated — maps to CRQ1]
25	
26	**Primary outcome measure**: [The single metric by which the primary analysis succeeds or fails]
27	
28	**Statistical test**: [Named test]
29	
30	**Decision rule**: The primary analysis is considered to support H1 if [specific criterion — effect size, p-value, CI — stated here].
31	
32	---
33	
34	## 2. Secondary Analyses
35	
36	Secondary analyses address additional questions that are pre-specified but not the primary focus. They are fully pre-registered but interpreted with the understanding that they were designed to explore, not definitively test.
37	
38	| Analysis ID | Question | Test | Outcome measure | Decision rule |
39	|-------------|---------|------|-----------------|---------------|
40	| SA-01 | | | | |
41	| SA-02 | | | | |
42	
43	---
44	
45	## 3. Sensitivity Analyses
46	
47	Sensitivity analyses test whether primary findings hold under alternative analytical choices. They are pre-specified here so they cannot be selectively reported.
48	
49	| Analysis ID | What varies | Baseline choice | Alternative choice | Expected direction of change |
50	|-------------|------------|-----------------|-------------------|------------------------------|
51	| SEN-01 | | | | |
52	
53	---
54	
55	## 4. Exploratory Analyses
56	
57	Exploratory analyses are hypothesis-generating. They are conducted on the data and their findings are stated as hypotheses for future confirmatory work. They are NOT used to revise the conclusions of the confirmatory analyses above.
58	
59	[List exploratory analyses planned before data examination. Additional exploratory analyses discovered during analysis are logged in `lab_notebook.md` with timestamps.]
60	
61	---
62	
63	## 5. Hypothesis Register
64	
65	Every confirmatory hypothesis is listed here with its full specification. The pre-registration file in `protocol/registration/` records the timestamp.
66	
67	### Hypothesis Format
68	
69	```
70	ID:           H[module].[number]
71	Registered:   YYYY-MM-DD (commit hash: [hash])
72	Question:     [one sentence — specific, directional, falsifiable]
73	H1:           [what we expect to find]
74	H0:           [what the world looks like if H1 is false]
75	Test:         [named statistical test]
76	Assumptions:  [what must hold for this test to be valid, and how verified]
77	Effect size:  [measure and minimum meaningful magnitude]
78	α:            [significance threshold — typically 0.05]
79	Power:        [target — typically 0.80]
80	Required N:   [from power analysis]
81	Available N:  [actual]
82	Powered:      [yes / no — if no, analysis is exploratory]
83	Correction:   [multiple comparison method if part of a family]
84	Confounds:    [named confounds and how controlled]
85	Data:         [specific dataset and split]
86	Script:       [path to analysis script]
87	```
88	
89	---
90	
91	## 6. Multiple Comparisons
92	
93	This project conducts [N] confirmatory hypothesis tests. Multiple comparison correction is applied as follows:
94	
95	**Family definition**: [Which tests form a family — tests addressing the same research question are one family]
96	
97	**Correction method**: [Benjamini-Hochberg FDR for large families; Bonferroni for small families where FWER control is required]
98	
99	**Corrected thresholds**: [List adjusted α per family]
100	
101	Tests are grouped into families here before analysis begins. Grouping is not adjusted after results are seen.
102	
103	| Family | Hypotheses included | Correction method | Adjusted α |
104	|--------|--------------------|--------------------|------------|
105	| F1 | | | |
106	
107	---
108	
109	## 7. Data Splits
110	
111	**Unit of analysis**: SCF control (~1,400 unique controls). Not mapping rows (280,000). All statistical tests assume independence at the control level. Mixed-effects modeling is required if mapping-level analysis is conducted.
112	
113	**Split strategy**: GroupKFold on SCF control IDs. No SCF control text appears in both train and test partitions. The DataSplitAgent asserts `len(set(train_control_ids) ∩ test_control_ids) == 0` before writing partition assignments to provenance. This assertion is logged and is a required Gate 2 artifact.
114	
115	**Leakage analysis**: Entity-level leakage (same control text in train and test via different mapping rows) is controlled by the GroupKFold strategy above. Temporal leakage: N/A — SCF corpus is a static snapshot. Feature leakage: [To be populated at Gate 2 — specific features identified after EDA].
116	
117	| Split | Purpose | Size | Grouped by | Touched when |
118	|-------|---------|------|-----------|--------------|
119	| Training | Model fitting | ~70% | SCF control ID | Phase 1 only |
120	| Validation | Tuning and model selection | ~15% | SCF control ID | Phase 1 only |
121	| Test | Final confirmatory evaluation | ~15% | SCF control ID | Once, at Gate 2 confirmatory run |
122	
123	**Cross-validation**: GroupKFold, k=5, groups=scf_control_id, on training split only.
124	
125	**Calibration sample:** The Phase 2 calibration sample for model selection (ADR-0013) is the validation split (15% of SCF controls). No additional partition is created for model selection. The 70/15/15 split accounts for all data partitions.
126	
127	---
128	
129	## 8. Missing Data Protocol
130	
131	**Expected missingness**: [anticipated rate per variable]
132	
133	**Mechanism assumption**: [MCAR / MAR / MNAR — and how assessed]
134	
135	**Handling strategy**: [complete case analysis / imputation method — specified before seeing missing data pattern]
136	
137	---
138	
139	## 9. Baseline Models
140	
141	The following baselines are defined and run before any complex model is evaluated. Results are locked.
142	
143	| Baseline | Description | Metric | Result (filled after baseline run) |
144	|----------|-------------|--------|-----------------------------------|
145	| Trivial | [e.g., majority class / mean] | | |
146	| Simple | [e.g., TF-IDF cosine / logistic regression] | | |
147	
148	No model is reported as performing "well" unless it beats the simple baseline on the primary metric by the pre-specified margin.
149	
150	---
151	
152	## 10. Model Selection Criteria
153	
154	Primary embedding model selection follows the two-phase protocol from ADR-0013. The selection criterion is fixed here and is not changed after any SCF data is examined.
155	
156	**Candidate model set diversity requirement:**
157	The candidate set must include at minimum: one or more general-purpose models (all-MiniLM, BGE, E5 family) plus two diversity anchors — (1) a model domain fine-tuned on legal/regulatory text; (2) a model from a different architecture class (sparse retrieval or substantially different training corpus). T-01 identifies specific models during Phase 0. Diversity anchor selection logged to Preregistration Ledger before Phase 1 runs. Phase 1 does not run without both anchors present.
158	
159	**Phase 1 — Benchmark evaluation (no SCF data):**
160	Candidate models evaluated on: (a) STS-B and SICK-R (general-purpose, coarse filter only — see Section 12 for domain shift limitation); (b) any regulatory/legal domain STS benchmarks identified by T-01 during Phase 0 — if found, added; if not found, absence documented and Phase 2 weighted more heavily; (c) SCF structural metadata anchor pairs: similarity anchors = STRM '= Equal To' pairs; dissimilarity anchors = STRM '∅ No Relation' pairs across unrelated domains. Selection criterion: composite of Spearman correlation on benchmark sets and anchor consistency score. Produces ranked shortlist of 3–5 models.
161	
162	**Phase 2 — Calibration sample evaluation (validation split):**
163	Shortlisted models evaluated on the validation split (15% of SCF controls) using:
164	- Silhouette score on the SCF control embedding space
165	- Anchor consistency score: proportion of '= Equal To' anchor pairs ranked above '∅ No Relation' anchor pairs
166	- Cross-model agreement: pairwise Spearman between models' pairwise similarity rankings across the full candidate set including diversity anchors
167	
168	**Primary model**: Model with highest composite score across all three Phase 2 criteria. Designation logged to Preregistration Ledger with cryptographic timestamp before Gate 2 split executes.
169	
170	**All other models**: Designated as secondary/sensitivity analyses at the same timestamp. Model selection is not revisited after Gate 2.
171	
172	**OPEN DECISION — DIVERGENCE-01:** Operationalization of model-STRM divergence metric. Must be selected and logged to the Preregistration Ledger before any STRM comparison results are examined. Candidate approaches: (A) Map STRM categories to ordinal numeric scale + Spearman rank correlation; (B) Threshold model similarity scores into STRM categories + agreement rate; (C) Polychoric correlation treating STRM as ordinal; (D) Rank preservation test. Selection criterion: choose the operationalization that makes fewest assumptions about STRM category spacing given the observed label distribution from EDA. This is a hard Gate 2 prerequisite — STRM comparison results cannot be examined before DIVERGENCE-01 is closed.
173	
174	---
175	
176	## 11. Reporting Standards
177	
178	- Effect size and confidence interval reported for every primary and secondary result
179	- Confidence intervals: 95%, bootstrap (10,000 resamples) unless parametric assumptions are verified
180	- Null results reported with effect size estimate and CI — "no significant difference" without a CI is not a finding
181	- All figures include uncertainty visualization (error bars, CI ribbons, or equivalent)
182	- Subgroup analyses pre-specified in Section 2; post-hoc subgroup analyses labeled exploratory
183	
184	---
185	
186	## 12. Limitations and Design Tradeoffs
187	
188	This section is required. It acknowledges known limitations of the design before any results are seen. It is written here so it cannot be omitted or softened after results are known.
189	
190	### Exploratory-Guided Hypothesis Selection
191	
192	The SAP is finalized at Gate 2, after exploratory analysis. This is intentional — specifying effect sizes before seeing the data produces authorial guesses presented as pre-specified thresholds (see issue #59 resolution). But this design has a cost: hypotheses entering Gate 2 are selected based on what looked tractable or promising in the exploratory phase. This is not traditional pre-registration. It is a two-stage adaptive design with a registration checkpoint between stages.
193	
194	**Nature of the bias:** Effect sizes for data-adaptive hypotheses are subject to inflation relative to theory-derived hypotheses. Hypotheses that looked uninteresting in exploratory analysis are less likely to be registered, even if they would have been pre-specified before data was seen.
195	
196	**Mitigation 1 — Multiple testing correction:** Benjamini-Hochberg FDR correction applied across all confirmatory hypotheses regardless of whether they are theory-derived or data-adaptive. Correction is applied to the full registered hypothesis set, not a subset selected after results.
197	
198	**Mitigation 2 — Mandatory null result reporting:** Every hypothesis that enters the confirmatory phase is reported in all outputs regardless of outcome. Selective reporting of significant findings is not permitted. The full hypothesis set and all test results appear in the Preregistration Ledger and in any published appendix.
199	
200	**Mitigation 3 — Epistemic status labeling:** All findings are labeled with their epistemic origin — `theory-derived` (pre-specified from literature/theory before any SCF data was seen), `data-adaptive` (registered before confirmatory test but generated from exploratory findings), or `exploratory` (not pre-registered). Labels appear in all outputs including publications. Readers assess inference strength for each finding independently.
201	
202	This limitation is disclosed in the methods section of all publications. The adaptive design is not a flaw to hide — it is the appropriate design for an autonomous iterative research system operating on data it has not yet seen. The mitigations above provide the transparency that makes adaptive findings interpretable.
203	
204	### Phase 1 Benchmark Domain Shift
205	
206	STS-B and SICK-R were built from everyday language corpora (image captions, news headlines). They do not test GRC-domain semantic distinctions. Phase 1 benchmark results are treated as a coarse filter only. Domain-appropriate evaluation relies on SCF structural metadata anchor pairs and Phase 2 calibration sample results. A human-labeled SCF control pair gold standard evaluation set does not currently exist and is designated as a post-publication community contribution milestone.
207	
208	### Anchor Consistency Circularity
209	
210	The anchor consistency score uses STRM-asserted relationship pairs ('= Equal To', '∅ No Relation') as weak supervision. STRM reliability is simultaneously under investigation as part of the secondary analysis. This creates a partial circularity: STRM assertions are used to validate the embedding while their reliability is being tested. The circularity is partial — silhouette score and cross-model agreement are independent of STRM — and is disclosed here prior to any results being examined.
211	
212	### STRM Inter-Rater Reliability
213	
214	STRM inter-rater reliability (Fleiss kappa on a re-rated sample) requires GRC domain expert raters. Two paths are available: (1) rater recruitment during Phase 0 — if selected, documented as a Gate 1 prerequisite; (2) if rater recruitment is infeasible, STRM reliability is unestimated. In path 2, the interpretation requiring evidence of STRM reliability is unavailable and model-STRM divergence cannot be attributed to annotator inconsistency vs. systematic annotation error. Path selection is logged to the Preregistration Ledger before Gate 1. Rater recruitment is also designated as a post-publication community contribution milestone.
215

content.text:
Now let me examine the code more carefully and compare it to the SAP specification.

**Code Analysis:**

The docstring in `agent.py:109-111` states:
> "Phase 1 runs all 5 candidates (3 general + 2 diversity anchors) and records F1 scores to inform Gate 2 model selection."

**Specification Analysis (SAP §10):**

Reading the actual specification in `03_statistical_analysis_plan.md`, lines 159-170 define two distinct phases:

1. **Phase 1 — Benchmark evaluation (line 159-160)**: "Candidate models evaluated on: (a) STS-B and SICK-R (general-purpose, coarse filter only...); (b) any regulatory/legal domain STS benchmarks...; (c) SCF structural metadata anchor pairs: similarity anchors = STRM '= Equal To' pairs; dissimilarity anchors = STRM '∅ No Relation' pairs across unrelated domains. Selection criterion: composite of Spearman correlation on benchmark sets and anchor consistency score. Produces ranked shortlist of 3–5 models."

2. **Phase 2 — Calibration sample evaluation (line 162-168)**: Uses the validation split (15% of SCF controls) with Silhouette score, Anchor consistency score, and Cross-model agreement. "Primary model: Model with highest composite score across all three Phase 2 criteria."

**Critical Discrepancy:**

The docstring says "records F1 scores to inform Gate 2 model selection" but the specification (lines 162-168) explicitly states that Phase 2 (the validation split evaluation) uses **Silhouette score, Anchor consistency score, and Cross-model agreement** — NOT F1 scores for model selection at Gate 2.

The code docstring at lines 121-122 does mention "F1 < 0.70 on STRM classification (evaluated by P1 agent after EDA)" but this is separate from the model selection process described in lines 109-111. The F1 score is a performance threshold check (triggering fine-tuning if failed), not the criterion used for model selection.

**Verification of Code Status:**

The `run()` method at line 130 raises `NotImplementedError`, indicating this is a Phase-0/1 stub. The docstring describes intended Phase 1 behavior but contains the inaccuracy.

**Severity Assessment:**

This is a genuine documentation defect in implemented code (the docstring is part of the codebase). While the actual implementation is stubbed, the docstring's inaccuracy could mislead developers building Phase 1 about which metrics drive model selection. This is medium severity — it's not a runtime bug in implemented logic (since nothing runs), but it contradicts the spec and could cause implementation errors.

input.title: Docstring describes incorrect Phase 1 evaluation criterion (F1 scores vs. benchmark composites)
input.verification: CONFIRMED DEFECT: The docstring at src/agents/embedding_agent/agent.py:109-111 states "Phase 1 runs all 5 candidates (3 general + 2 diversity anchors) and records F1 scores to inform Gate 2 model selection." This contradicts SAP §10 (lines 159-170) which specifies: Phase 1 evaluation uses (a) STS-B/SICK-R benchmarks, (b) regulatory domain benchmarks, (c) SCF structural metadata anchor pairs with selection criterion of "composite of Spearman correlation on benchmark sets and anchor consistency score" producing a "ranked shortlist of 3–5 models." Phase 2 (not Phase 1) performs validation split evaluation using Silhouette score, Anchor consistency score, and Cross-model agreement to select the primary model. F1 score (line 121-122) is a performance threshold (0.70) triggering fine-tuning, not a model selection criterion. The docstring misrepresents both what Phase 1 evaluates and what Gate 2 uses for model selection.
input.recommended_action: Revise lines 109-111 to read: "Phase 1 evaluates all 5 candidates (3 general + 2 diversity anchors) on benchmarks (STS-B, SICK-R, regulatory domain) and SCF structural metadata anchor pairs (STRM '= Equal To' and '∅ No Relation' pairs per SAP §10) using composite Spearman correlation and anchor consistency scores to produce a ranked shortlist for Phase 2 calibration." Add clarification at lines 121-123 that F1 evaluation is a separate performance quality gate (not model selection) executed post-Gate 2 and triggering domain fine-tuning if F1 < 0.70.
